narrative: OMX30 faces a conflicted setup Thursday morning. While the index trades +6.0% above its 200-day MA in an official bull regime, overnight signals are distinctly negative: European bourses closed lower (FTSE especially weak at -1.16%), global correlation turned negative (-0.500%), and oil's 4.67% drop despite supply-disruption headlines signals demand anxiety rather than geopolitical risk-on. Technically, oversold conditions (RSI 32.83, Stochastic in deep oversold) suggest mean-reversion potential, but ADX at 13.7—well below the 18 threshold—severely weakens our confidence in any bounce; we reduce magnitude by 30% and flag this as a low-conviction environment. US 10Y yield spike to 4.418% (up 147bp) will support bank NIMs but compress tech and real estate valuations. The risk/reward tilts slightly negative.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens Thursday under pressure from a weak European close and negative overnight correlation. FTSE's 1.16% drop is the sharpest signal; DAX and CAC also red. Key tension: crude oil fell 4.67% despite headlines about supply disruption—classic risk-off behavior suggesting demand fears trump geopolitical premiums. Technically, oversold stochastics (K=3.8) and RSI 32.83 could tempt mean-reversion trades toward 3123–3140 range, but ADX at 13.7 is too weak to trust the reversal; cut position sizing by 30%. Watch 3085 lower Bollinger as hard floor. Sector calls: Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) modestly supported by 10Y yield strength; Industrials (Volvo, Atlas Copco) benefit from USD/SEK at 9.30 but hamstrung by oil cost anxiety. Avoid Tech/Real Estate into yield strength. Key release: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET could reignite volatility. If 3100 breaks, target 3050.
drivers: Weak European close (DAX -0.27%, CAC -0.39%, FTSE -1.16%) with negative correlation signal (-0.500%)
Oil down 4.67% to 112.5 despite headline about supply disruption — demand concern signals risk-off
OMX30 oversold (RSI 32.83, Stochastic K=3.8) but ADX weak at 13.7 limits reversal confidence
Yield spike (US 10Y +147bp) compresses valuations; tech/real estate exposed while bank NIM benefit offset by macro caution
USD/SEK strength (+0.46%) supports exporters but insufficient to overcome broad sentiment headwinds

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +147bp to 4.418% expands NIM outlook; however, broader macro caution limits enthusiasm.
Oil down 4.67% raises input cost uncertainty; USD/SEK strength (+0.46%) provides partial offset for exporters but insufficient.
Gold +1.14% to 4597 and copper +1.17% to 5.947 provide direct revenue tailwinds; Boliden particularly supported.
NASDAQ flat overnight but yield spike compresses DCF valuations; weak ADX reduces sentiment confidence.
Yield spike to 4.418% increases refinancing costs and caps valuations; defensive bid limited by macro caution.
Oil down suggests some spending relief, but weak EUR/SEK (+0.07%) slightly raises sourcing cost pressure.
Risk-off sentiment and yield compression may trigger modest defensive rotation into quality; still dampened by low ADX.

watchList.reason: Gold +1.14% and copper +1.17% are direct revenue catalysts; outperform on commodity strength even in risk-off.
USD/SEK +0.46% supports export competitiveness but oil down 4.67% raises input-cost concern; mixed signals.
10Y yield +147bp widens NIM; only modest upside if macro weakness persists.
Tech sentiment weak; yield spike compresses valuations; NASDAQ flat overnight offers no support.
Weak EUR/SEK and modest oil relief create cross-currents; avoid until volatility subsides.

keyLevels.support: 3085 (lower Bollinger band); 3050 (short-term floor if oversold reversal fails)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish data releases Thursday. US Jobless Claims (initial & continuing) at 14:30 CET is key macro event; elevated VIX (18.81) signals market will react sharply to miss/beat.
topRisk: US Jobless Claims worse than expected could trigger flight-to-safety, pushing OMX30 below 3085 support. Trade-war escalation (per FT headlines on 'Does trade cause peace?') remains tail risk if UK/EU tensions spike post-Labour.
technicalSummary: Bull regime intact: +6.0% above 200-MA, but -1.7% below 50-MA signals weakening momentum
RSI 32.83 is neutral leaning oversold; Stochastic K=3.8 D=8.1 in deep oversold territory — classic mean-reversion setup
MACD below signal (hist=-11.762) is bearish; ADX 13.7 is critically weak, reducing confidence in any reversal by 30%
Bollinger %B=0.19 at lower half; width 7.8% indicates low volatility compression — be ready for squeeze breakout
ROC-90 +6.3% shows strong 90-day momentum despite near-term weakness; this supports 200-MA support floor
52-week positioning at 74% of range with -6.6% from high suggests range-bound consolidation risk
